steps.title: Use the origin shortcut
steps.content: Both equations have no constant term: $y=-\frac{1}{9}x$ and $y=\frac{1}{2}x$. So both lines pass through $(0,0)$.

steps.title: Confirm algebraically
steps.content: Set the right sides equal: $$-\frac{x}{9}=\frac{x}{2}.$$ Multiplying by $18$ gives $-2x=9x$, so $11x=0$ and $x=0$.

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content: $0$ is correct. The other choices are nonzero $x$-values, but these two distinct lines through the origin intersect at $x=0$.
